title: Enums 1
description: Enums allow you to define a type by enumerating its possible variants.
instructions: Complete the enum definition so the code compiles.
hints: Enum variants are listed inside the enum body, separated by commas.
Simple variants without data are just names.
Define them as: Quit, Echo, Move, ChangeColor
explanation: Enums in Rust let you define a type that can be one of several variants. Each variant is a distinct value of the enum type. You access variants using `EnumName::Variant` syntax. The `#[derive(Debug)]` attribute lets you print enum values with `{:?}`.
